claimText: Flue Gas Desulphurisation (FGD) is a process used to remove Sulphur Dioxide (SO2) from the exhaust gases of coal-fired power plants.
evidenceExcerpt: FGD involves the removal of Sulphur Dioxide (SO2) from the exhaust gases of coal-fired power plants.
examUse: Statement-based question on the purpose and target pollutant of FGD systems.
distractorHooks: Targeting Nitrogen Oxides (NOx) or Carbon Monoxide (CO) instead of SO2
Used in natural gas plants instead of coal-fired plants

claimText: The FGD process utilizes a scrubbing technique that typically involves sodium-based or calcium-based alkaline reagents.
evidenceExcerpt: It uses a scrubbing technique that typically involves a sodium- or calcium-based alkaline reagent.
examUse: Statement-based question on the chemical mechanism and reagents used in FGD.
distractorHooks: Using acidic reagents instead of alkaline reagents
Using iron-based or copper-based reagents instead of sodium or calcium

claimText: A recent study recommended limiting the mandatory installation of FGD systems to coal plants using imported coal or high-sulphur coal (greater than 0.5 percent sulphur) rather than all plants.
evidenceExcerpt: A recent study recommended limiting the FGD mandate to plants using imported or high-sulphur (>0.5%) coal rather than all plants.
examUse: Current affairs statement-based question on environmental policy changes regarding coal plants.
distractorHooks: Mandating FGD for all coal plants without exception
Limiting FGD to plants using domestic low-sulphur coal
